LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF PERSONNEL ADMINISTRATION'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT IS GRANTED AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 2 BECAUSE THERE IS NO STATUTORY AUTHORITY FOR SUCH A CONTRACT. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 1 BECAUSE NEITHER THE COMPLAINT NOR WHAT MAY BE JUDICIALLY NOTICED ESTABLISHES THAT PLAINTIFFS RECEIVED GENERALLY EQUIVALENT CHARGES. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR# 8189

LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF PERSONNEL ADMINISTRATION'S MOTION TO FILE AMENDED ANSWER IS DENIED AS TO PROPOSED DEFENSE 20 BECAUSE DEFENDANT'S DECLARATION DOES NOT EXPLAIN WHY IT IS BEING PROPOSED SO LATE, BECAUSE IT COULD HAVE BEEN ASSERTED LONG AGO BECAUSE IT IS TOO VAGUE TO ALERT PLAINTIFF'S AS TO WHAT IS CLAIMED; AND BECAUSE GIVEN ALL THIS IT WOULD PREJUDICE PLAINTIFF'S COMING SO CLOSE AS IT DOES TO TRIAL AND AFTER DISCOVERY HAS CLOSED. PROPOSED DEFENSES 18 AND 19 ARE MOOT IN LIGHT OF THE RULING ON THE MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. THE MOTION IS OTHERWISE GRANTED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR# 8189

THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: THE COURT HEREBY ORDERS THAT: 1) FOR THE REASONS SET FORTH IN THE COURT'S STATEMENT OF DECISION, THE COURT FINDS NO VIOLATION OF GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 19849.18; 2) THE COMPLAINT FOR INJUNCTIVE RELIEF ALLEGING A VIOLATION OF GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 19849.18 IS HEREBY DENIED IN ITS ENTIRETY; AND 3) DEFENDANTS SHALL BE AWARDED COSTS AS THE PREVAILING PARTY IN THIS ACTION. Off Calendar May-05-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: ATTY. GREG MCLEAN ADAM OF CARROLL, BURDICK & MCDONOUGH LLP, TEL. 743-2534, FOR PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. CHRISTOPHER E. THOMAS, LABOR RELATIONS COUNSEL OF THE DEPT. OF PERSONNEL ADMINISTRATION, TEL. (916) 324-0512, FOR THE DEFENDANT AND MOVING PARTY CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF PERSONNEL ADMINISTRATION.

RULING: A R G U E D. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF PERSONNEL ADMINISTRATION'S Motion To File Amended Answer IS DENIED AS TO PROPOSED DEFENSE 20 BECAUSE DEFENDANT'S DECLARATION DOES NOT EXPLAIN WHY IT IS BEING PROPOSED SO LATE, BECAUSE IT COULD HAVE BEEN ASSERTED LONG AGO BECAUSE IT IS TOO VAGUE TO ALERT PLAINTIFF'S AS TO WHAT IS CLAIMED; AND BECAUSE GIVEN ALL THIS IT WOULD PREJUDICE PLAINTIFF'S COMING SO CLOSE AS IT DOES TO TRIAL AND AFTER DISCOVERY HAS CLOSED. PROPOSED DEFENSES 18 AND 19 ARE MOOT IN LIGHT OF THE RULING ON THE MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. THE MOTION IS OTHERWISE GRANTED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR# 8189 =301/PJB/AL
